summary: Closed, unmerged PR that introduced a shared MACRO_ft_OnItemPickup helper for the repeated fighter OnItemPickup/OnItemCatch callback pattern, renamed many ftdata callback table entries from raw func_* symbols to fighter-specific *_OnItemPickup names, and matched or partially matched several fighter files by replacing assembly/handwritten duplicate C with macro invocations. The PR body only says "and various other fighter function matching" and there were no review comments in the slice, so intent and acceptance rationale are lightly evidenced.
what_changed: Added MACRO_ft_OnItemPickup(FTNAME, param1, param2) in src/melee/ft/fighter.h. The macro emits FTNAME##_OnItemPickup(HSD_GObj* fighterObj, BOOL bool), checks the held item with func_8026B2B4, switches on func_8026B320, calls func_80070FB4 with third arguments 1, 0, 2, or 3 for cases 1 through 4, and optionally calls func_80070C48 when the BOOL argument is true.

Replaced repeated per-fighter OnItemPickup implementations with macro invocations, including ftCFalcon, ftDonkey, ftDrMario, ftRoy, ftFalco, ftFox, ftGanon, ftGKoopa, ftIcelimber, ftKoopa, ftLuigi, ftMario, ftMars, ftNess, ftPeach, ftPichu, ftPikachu, ftSamus, ftSeak, ftYoshi, ftZakoBoy, ftZakoGirl, and ftZelda.

Renamed ftdata.s callback table entries for ft_OnItemPickup and ft_OnItemCatch from raw func_* labels to descriptive fighter callback names such as ftMario_OnItemPickup, ftFox_OnItemPickup, ftCFalcon_OnItemPickup, ftDonkey_OnItemPickup, ftGameWatch_OnItemPickup, ftGanon_OnItemPickup, ftRoy_OnItemPickup, ftZakoBoy_OnItemPickup, ftZakoGirl_OnItemPickup, and ftGKoopa_OnItemPickup.

Kept or marked exceptions where the generic macro did not apply. The ftdata.s diff comments note that the macro does not work for ftKirby_OnItemPickup, ftLink_OnItemPickup, ftPurin_OnItemPickup, ftCLink_OnItemPickup, and that ftMewtwo_OnItemPickup is a stub function.
Deleted asm/melee/ft/chara/ftCaptain/ftfalcon.s entirely in the diff excerpt, indicating a large ftCaptain/Falcon assembly removal associated with C matching already present or added in this PR.
Touched many fighter headers to rename prototypes from func_* to *_OnItemPickup and to use BOOL for the pickup/catch flag parameter.
Expanded SpecialAttrs_Kirby in fighter.h with fields from 0x2294 through 0x22E8 and changed SpecialAttrs_Kirby.x222C and SpecialAttrs_Seak.x222C from u32 to s32.
Added smaller fighter matching work outside the macro, including changed C for Kirby, GameWatch, Seak, Samus, Peach, Yoshi, and Zelda, plus minor asm symbol renames for Mewtwo and Purin.
smart_moves: Identified a highly repetitive callback body across many fighters and converted it into a parameterized macro instead of carrying many near-identical C functions.
Preserved matching-sensitive differences with two macro parameters: param1 controls the second argument to func_80070FB4, and param2 controls the second argument to the optional func_80070C48 call.
Used token pasting, FTNAME##_OnItemPickup, to enforce consistent symbol names while keeping each function addressable by ftdata.s tables.
Applied the renamed symbols directly in ft_OnItemPickup and ft_OnItemCatch tables, making callback table semantics easier to search than raw func_* addresses.

Did not force every fighter into the macro: the diff explicitly labels macro exceptions such as Link, CLink, Kirby, Purin, and Mewtwo, which is useful evidence that the pattern was checked per fighter rather than blindly applied.
Changed function prototypes to BOOL where the argument is used as a boolean gate, improving type intent without changing the observed callback ABI.
decomp_lessons: When many fighter callbacks differ only by a small constant or boolean behavior, a macro can reduce source duplication while still producing individual named functions required by callback tables.
Callback tables are valuable places to propagate semantic names once a repeated pattern is understood; renaming table entries from func_* to *_OnItemPickup improves future symbol discovery.
Do not assume all fighters share the generic item pickup pattern. Link, CLink, Kirby, Purin, and stub-style callbacks such as Mewtwo may need explicit implementations or assembly retention.

For matching C generated by macros, keep parameter choices visible at each invocation, e.g. MACRO_ft_OnItemPickup(ftMars, 0, 1) or MACRO_ft_OnItemPickup(ftPikachu, 0, 0), so reviewers can compare per-character behavior quickly.

Struct signedness and field layout adjustments may be necessary alongside function matching; this PR changed some fighter special attribute fields from u32 to s32 and added Kirby fields through 0x22E8.
Large asm deletion should be paired with descriptive symbol renames and table updates so later decomp work can trace which assembly routines were matched.
naming_conventions: Use fighter-specific callback names of the form ft<Name>_OnItemPickup rather than raw func_* labels.
The macro emits FTNAME##_OnItemPickup, so invocations use existing project fighter prefixes such as ftCFalcon, ftGKoopa, ftMars, ftSeak, and ftZakoBoy.
OnItemCatch table entries may reuse the same *_OnItemPickup functions when the code path is shared, as shown by ft_OnItemCatch using many of the same renamed callbacks.
Prototype the pickup flag as BOOL when the parameter is only tested for truthiness.
The diff preserves existing project spelling quirks such as ftIcelimber_OnItemPickup.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Macro-generated C was used as a matching tactic for repeated item callback routines, replacing functions of roughly 26-35 lines each with one macro invocation.
The macro shape mirrors the assembly control flow: early held-item state check via func_8026B2B4, switch on func_8026B320, four explicit cases, and a final conditional side-effect call.
Different fighters matched by changing only constants: common invocations use (1, 1), Marth/Roy-style examples use (0, 1), and Pichu/Pikachu/Samus/Zako-style examples use (0, 0).
Symbol-only asm changes renamed remaining assembly labels for Mewtwo and Purin to ftMewtwo_OnItemPickup and ftPurin_OnItemPickup even where the macro was not used.
The PR removed a full ftCaptain/ftfalcon.s assembly file and reduced/rewrote assembly in Kirby, GameWatch, Seak, Samus, Peach, Yoshi, Zelda, and other fighter asm files.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR was closed with merged_at null, so this record should not treat the macro approach as accepted upstream solely from this PR.
follow_up_queries: Check later PRs for whether MACRO_ft_OnItemPickup or a revised equivalent was merged after PR #399 closed unmerged.
Audit fighters marked with "macro doesn't work" in ftdata.s to document why their OnItemPickup behavior differs.
Search for ftIcelimber_OnItemPickup spelling in current code before reusing this naming convention.
Compare the macro-generated functions against decomp.me scratch references mentioned in comments, especially the ftCFalcon OnItemPickup scratch.
Verify whether the ftCaptain/ftfalcon.s deletion corresponds to fully matched C in current master or only this abandoned branch.
